title: Account & Payments
body: Account & Payments All endpoints require Authorization: Bearer {JWT}. Amounts are in micro-USDT (integer, 6 decimal places — divide by 1,000,000 to get USDT). -- Balance Field | Description | -------|-------------| free | Available — can be staked or withdrawn | locked | In open orders — released on

title: Authentication
body: Authentication Carnot uses EVM wallet signature challenges. Sign a backend-issued nonce to prove wallet ownership; receive a JWT for all subsequent API calls. title: WebSocket Events
body: WebSocket Events Carnot uses Socket.IO v4 over WSS. Price ticks, order results, and the price grid are all pushed — no polling needed. title: Order Engine
body: Order Engine The order engine validates incoming bets, tracks them against the live price stream, and settles them within milliseconds of the window closing. Order Submission Validation User calls POST /api/orders with the full cell object (including gridSignature) and a stake amount. Four checks mu

title: Real-Time Price Feed
body: Real-Time Price Feed All price data originates from the Binance Futures aggTrade stream, dispatched to four internal consumers. Source One subscription per market (btcusdt, ethusdt, solusdt). The backend keeps the latest trade per market in memory, deduplicated by tradeId. Reconnects automatically w

title: Fee Structure
body: Fee Structure All fees are in basis points (bps). 1 bps = 0.01%. Fee Parameters Parameter | Value (bps) | % | Description | ---|---|---|---| KEEPERFEEBPS | 25 | 0.25% | Paid to the keeper per batch | PROTOCOLFEEBPS | 100 | 1.00% | Paid to protocol treasury (pool-gains batches only) | PLATFORMEDGEBPS

title: Price Bands & Outcomes
body: Price Bands & Outcomes Every bet is placed on a price band — a fixed interval for a specific market and 5-second window. Band Definition Field | Description | -------|-------------| lowerPrice | Inclusive lower bound | upperPrice | Exclusive upper bound (lowerPrice + priceCellSize) | Fixed band widt

title: The Price Grid
body: The Price Grid The grid is a 10×9 matrix of tradeable cells rebroadcast every 250 ms per market. Each cell is a unique (time window, price band) pair. Dimensions Dimension | Value | Meaning | -----------|-------|---------| gridNX | 10 | Time columns | gridNY | 9 | Price rows | Column width | 5 secon

title: Settlement Batches
body: Settlement Batches All settled orders within a 1-minute window are aggregated into a settlement batch — a self-contained record verified by a single ZK proof. Batch Window Each batch covers exactly 1 minute (60 seconds) of settled orders, aligned to wall-clock boundaries. Orders settle after the pol

body: Trade Lifecycle States a trade moves through from submission to on-chain claim: -- States PENDING Accepted by the backend, written to the database. title: Pool Solvency Model
body: Pool Solvency Model The LP pool is the counterparty for every bet. These invariants keep it solvent. Each cell carries an HMAC-SHA256 gridSignature committing the band, wind

title: What is Carnot
body: What is Carnot Carnot is a permissionless, ZK-verified prediction market on Solana. You pick a price band, bet on whether the settlement candle closes inside it within a 5-second window, and get your result in under 10 seconds. Every batch of settled orders is committed on-chain as a Groth16 ZK proo

title: Instructions Reference
body: Instructions Reference All instructions are Anchor-IDL-compatible, callable via the Anchor TypeScript client or any Solana transaction builder. Summary Instruction | Caller | Description | -------------|--------|-------------| admininit | Admin | Initialize global state — keeper pubkey, fee rates, b

title: Program Overview
body: Program Overview carnotengine is a Solana program built with Anchor 0.32.0 in Rust. It handles fund custody, ZK proof verification, batch settlement, and payout distribution. Program Identity Property | Value | ----------|-------| Program Name | carnotengine | Program ID | carCrmy6qN8tRgvUp9v6JrfUux

title: Settlement Math
body: Settlement Math These formulas are verified by ZK constraints (C4, D1, D3, D4) and re-enforced by the Solana program. Live parameters: keeperfeebps = 25, protocolfeebps = 100. Gross Payout (Per Winner) multiplierbps = 10000 means 1× (return of stake only). multiplierbps = 15000 means 1.5×. Bounded t

title: Aggregator Circuit
body: Aggregator Circuit For large batches, running the full settlement circuit over thousands of trades in a single prover invocation would take too long. Carnot solves this with chunked aggregation: split the batch into chunks, prove each chunk in parallel, then combine into a single final proof. How It

title: Circuit Constraints
body: import { DocsOracleBindingDiagram } from "../../components/WorkflowEmbeds"; Circuit Constraints The Carnot settlement circuit verifies 28 constraints across 6 groups (A–F). Any single failure aborts proof generation — the Solana program will reject the settlement transaction. Circuit source: carnot-

title: On-Chain Verification
body: On-Chain Verification After the keeper generates a Groth16 proof, it submits it via verifyandsettle on carCrmy6qN8tRgvUp9v6JrfUuxroGrKdndUdwMMNumS. This instruction atomically verifies the proof and finalizes the batch. Every step is atomic — any failure reverts with no state change. Execution Steps

title: ZK Settlement — Overview
body: import { DocsSettlementPipelineDiagram } from "../../components/WorkflowEmbeds"; ZK Settlement — Overview Every settlement batch is verified by a Groth16 ZK proof before any funds move. The operator cannot produce a valid proof for an incorrectly computed batch — the math makes it impossible. Why ZK

title: SP1 zkVM & Groth16
body: SP1 zkVM & Groth16 Carnot's settlement proofs are generated by SP1 (Succinct Labs), a general-purpose ZK virtual machine. How SP1 Works SP1 compiles Rust to RISC-V and proves, cryptographically, that a specific execution trace was carried out correctly. The circuit is just a normal Rust program — au
